content:
There are some issues with dead links on opencpn.org and User Manual.
I have advised Kathi of the changes you all made.
I have corrected all the links in the User Manual that failed.
https://opencpn.org/wiki/dokuwiki/doku.php?id=opencpn:dev_manual
https://opencpn.org/wiki/dokuwiki/doku.php?id=opencpn:developer_manual

I have reports of some Dev Users missing some things that were on the old website Dev Manual.
https://opencpn.org/wiki/dokuwiki/doku.php?id=opencpn:developer_manual
More on that later.

Additionally the new indexes have developed such that there are major heading or topics that have no text page, and are just placeholders so that they cannot be used to provide links. I think this really should be improved so that the title or main pages have some brief textual explanation, rather than picking some lower page for a link!
This occurs primarily in the Development and Plugin Dev Manuals.

Also some times when the top page is opened, the index does not also open and users get lost because they do not see where to go next. I think we should have the index open automatically so it can be seen and selected.

Also the first page of the manual should have the major links to the main topics so that if the index does not open, users will have a ready selection of the main topics. This would apply to both the Plugin Dev manual and Dev manual.

That did a lot!
I seem to have VDR 1.3.22 and Logbook 1.4.28 in addition to the newer ones. Should I remove those and commit and push now?

I tried removing those old files locally and committed the change. then tried to push it up and I was asked to pulll.
So I pulled and there are now conflicts again. I did it wrong, I should have pulled first. Then removed. the duplicates.
I guess I should start over again.

Alec Leamas11:42 AM
OK, that master branch is seriously broken. You need to reset it. Do:
$ git checkout -b foo
$ git remote update upstream
$ git fetch -f upstream master:master
$ git checkout master
This will give you a fresh, rebased version of the master branch.

Alec, I've totally screwed it up again. I have to stop for the time being, and come back to this later.
Sorry, I wanted to get VDR and Logbook updated and submitted to master catalog, but its not going to happen today. Thanks for all the help. I am going to dive into this some more later.
This topic was moved here from #general > Dev Index & Links by Alec Leamas.
